description: Canonical topic taxonomy for the Ryan Realty marketing brain. Used by competitor-recon classifier, generate-briefs producer-mix mapper, and content_performance aggregation. Read by lib/marketing-brain/topic-taxonomy.ts at runtime. Editing this file changes the classifier output domain — re-run any in-flight classification after edits.

topics.description: Per-listing marketing: just-listed, just-sold, coming-soon, open house, under contract, price improvement, featured listing. The single property is the subject.
examples: Just listed on Awbrey Butte — 4-bed, 3-bath, 3,200 sqft
Open house Saturday 1-3pm — 567 SW Canyon Blvd
Sold over asking: a quiet downtown stunner that found its people

topics.description: Bend / Deschutes / Central Oregon market stats, trends, snapshots, monthly reports. Includes explainer treatments of market data. Distinguished from national_housing_news by geographic scope (local) and from national_economy by content type (real estate stats specifically).
examples: Bend April 2026 market update: median price up 3.2%, MoS holding at 4.1
Why Bend's months of supply matters more than the headline price number
Three numbers that tell you what kind of market Bend is in this week

topics.description: Fed moves, NAR reports, Case-Shiller, mortgage rate changes, migration data, national housing-policy news with a Bend interpretation layer. The story is national; the angle is what it means locally.
examples: Fed cut rates 25bp — here is what changes for Bend buyers in the next 30 days
NAR's new commission rule lands today. The plain-English version for Bend
Case-Shiller print: Bend is decoupled from the national index. Why

topics.description: CPI, jobs report, GDP, Fed dot plot, regional economic indicators interpreted for housing-buyer decision-making. Distinguished from national_housing_news by primary subject (the economy, not housing) and from market_data by scope (national, not local).
examples: CPI dropped again. What it tells you about mortgage rates heading into summer
Oregon jobs report: Deschutes County added 1,200 jobs. The housing-demand signal
When the dollar strengthens, this is what happens to West Coast home prices

topics.description: Bend / Redmond / Sisters / Sunriver community happenings: events, restaurant openings, school district news, business spotlights, civic decisions affecting residents. NOT lifestyle (which is brand-of-place) and NOT listings.
examples: Mt. Bachelor opens uphill access this weekend. Here is the schedule
New restaurant on Galveston: Hop Pad opens June 1
Bend-La Pine school district passes the bond. What it funds and when

topics.description: Brand-of-place content. Outdoor recreation, dining, breweries, scenery, seasonal moments — the why-people-move-here story. Distinguished from local_community (which is news/info) by emotional register: lifestyle is aspirational + sensory + atmospheric.
examples: First snow on Bachelor. The morning the season turns
Tin Pig is the Bend lunch spot worth the wait — here is why
Smith Rock at golden hour. A reason to live in Central Oregon

topics.description: First-time buyer 101, financing, inspection process, appraisal, closing, what-to-look-for-in-a-home, contingencies. The reader is a prospective buyer learning the process.
examples: First-time buyer in Bend: the actual seven-step process, no jargon
What an inspection report looks like — and the four items that should give you pause
How appraisal gaps work and what to do when one happens to your offer

topics.description: Pricing strategy, listing prep, offers + negotiations, sellers-disclosure obligations, closing process from the seller side. The reader is a homeowner considering or executing a sale.
examples: Pricing strategy in a 4-MoS market: three pricing models and when each works
What buyers actually notice on the first walkthrough — and the four cheap fixes that matter
Multiple offers on your Bend home: how to read them past the headline price

topics.description: Broker day-in-the-life, team intro, deal stories (without specifics that would breach confidentiality), brokerage culture, how-the-business-actually-works. The brokerage is the subject.
examples: A Tuesday at Ryan Realty: four showings, one offer, one impossible kitchen
How we screen Bend listings before we put them in front of a buyer
The hardest deal we closed this year. What it took

topics.description: Week-in-review, month-in-review, year-in-review, milestone announcements, deal-of-the-quarter. Looks backward over a window to surface what mattered.
examples: Bend market — May 2026 in five numbers
Year-in-review: the four trends that defined Central Oregon real estate this year
Top three Bend listings we toured this month and why each is worth a look

topics.description: Press features, awards, milestones, brag stats, brokerage-level brand moments. The brand itself is the subject.
examples: Featured this week in Cascade Business News — the long view on Bend brokerages
Sold a record 47 homes in Q1. Here is what made the difference
Why Matt founded Ryan Realty — and what that means for how we work

topics.description: Catch-all for content that does not cleanly fit a bucket. Classifier MUST flag with topic_confidence below 0.6 and route to Sonnet review if confidence is low. Posts that consistently land here signal a missing taxonomy bucket — review monthly.
